message:
perf(btree): identity-skip memmove in defrag when cell already at target offset

Before the fix, delete defrag and interior-slot repair called
`page_bytes.copy_within(ptr..ptr + size, new_content_offset)` even when
`new_content_offset == ptr` (cell already at the correct packed
position). The `copy_within` / `__memmove_avx_unaligned_erms` band was
still visible after c951a913 because every per-cell move went through
the memmove intrinsic even for the no-op case.

Adding `if new_content_offset != ptr { ... }` at all 4 defrag sites
(table-leaf delete defrag, interior-append defrag, generic-page defrag,
separator-repair defrag) skips the memmove entirely for cells already
at their compact position — which is common when a delete removes a
higher-offset cell, since cells below the deletion gap don't need to
move.

Hyperfine perf-update-delete 10000 10 both (10 runs, same machine):
  pre  271.2 ms ± 4.1 ms
  post 261.0 ms ± 3.2 ms   (1.04x faster, 3.8% speedup)

Isomorphism: `copy_within(a..b, a)` is a no-op; skipping it preserves
page bytes exactly. `ptrs[i]` is still assigned to `new_content_offset`
on both paths.

bd-1dp9.6.7.11

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
